programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack and Chrissy agree to take over Janet's babysitting job for a night; to their dismay, they face a crying baby, no TV and a locked liquor cabinet.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Chrissy gets a film camera through her boyfriend to make some home movies; the projector is put to deviant use when Larry acquires a porn movie.

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race
programme.desc: The queens put their design skills to the test in a double ball challenge, stomping the runway in 42 fashion looks.

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race
programme.desc: Michelle Visage's online shopping has gotten out of hand; in this design challenge, the queens are tasked with making outfits from all the junk she has ordered; Taraji P. Henson guest judges.

programme.desc: The queens don shoulder pads and flex their acting muscles in a campy '80s soap opera; LGBT activist TS Madison guest judges.

programme.desc: The queens sing and dance in '60s girls groups The Rupremes, The Runettes and The Shangrulas; comedian Alec Mapa guest judges.

programme.desc: The queens must lead Drag Con panel discussions about the multi-faceted world of men; dating, fatherhood and toxic masculinity; comedian Nicole Byer judges.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: Mama describes parenthood to Naomi's childbirth class.

programme.sub-title: There Is Nothing Like the Dames
programme.desc: Mama (Vicki Lawrence Schultz) hopes to become a member of an exclusive women's club.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: Naomi and Vint's (Dorothy Lyman, Ken Berry) baby arrives early.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.sub-title: Vint and the Kids Move In
programme.desc: Evicted Vint shows up with his children.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: Vint and Naomi announce wedding plans.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: While Vint is at his stag party, the women have a get-together that turns disastrous.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: Naomi and Vint wind up on Mama's doorstep after they're swindled out of their housing investment.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: The Harper family competes on "Family Feud.".

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: Mama and Eunice share confidences in jail.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: Mama goes to work for a travel agency.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: Tempers flare after Sonia finds out Buzz has been given a later curfew on the night of the homecoming dance.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: Mama takes a weekend trip with an old beau.

programme.title: Mama's Family
programme.desc: Mama runs out to replace Fran's dress after she accidentally burns a hole in it.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack agrees to work for Janet in the flower shop, but he doesn't take the job seriously, which causes problems in their relationship.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: The roommates plan a dinner for the Ropers, but their evening is ruined when a blind guy who Jack knew in the Navy shows up.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: A pregnant woman claims Jack is the father of her unborn child.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack discovers what appears to be marijuana plants in Roper's garden.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Chrissy's minister father pays an early visit.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: The roommates take the Ropers' parakeet overnight.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: An old girlfriend shows up at Jack's surprise birthday party.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack breaks a date with his girlfriend to keep one with a new romantic interest.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Janet convinces Mrs. Roper to join her in a nude beach protest.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Roper thinks the candid diary Chrissy is transcribing is her own.

programme.title: Three's Company
programme.desc: Jack and Chrissy compete in a test of willpower.
